rationale.R1.2: Section contains causal/mechanistic claims (e.g., self-consistency worsens calibration on long-horizon tasks due to trajectory-level systematic bias; per-step propagation avoids this failure mode) that form a logical chain requiring consistency checking.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple specific quantitative claims are asserted inline (38% avg ECE reduction, 47% on ToolBench, Brier improvement of 0.12, <1.5pp success-rate delta) with no pointer to tables or figures — alignment between these numbers and actual experimental evidence is unverifiable from the text alone.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look at this paragraph' is a natural-language cue consistent with prose-level review; writing quality, sentence flow, and clarity are plausibly in scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking me to check whether the quantitative claims and experimental logic are internally sound (substance/consistency review), or are you asking for feedback on writing clarity and presentation quality (prose review)?
